notes: FROZEN (agent=yes, daemon=incapable, driver=ready/moot). Kiro's auth IS a subscription (kiro-cli login / Google/AWS Builder ID, kiro-cli whoami, /stats + TUI footer credits), but NONE of that subscription/credit/rate-limit state reaches the .jsonl the daemon tails — the 2.5.1 vocabulary is Prompt/AssistantMessage/ToolResults/Clear with no model/token/cost/credit/plan field; the data lives only in the <uuid>.json sidecar's metering_usage, which the daemon does not read. The kiro adapter wires NEITHER of irrlicht's two existing transports for this signal: claudecode's out-of-band statusline POST hook (statusline.go) nor codex's in-band JSONL rate_limits field (codex/parser.go:374-423). So no per-session RateLimitSnapshot (subscription vs API-key reading) can ever be produced for a kiro session; the daemon fundamentally cannot observe this row today (incapable, not bug — no trace exists). No lifecycle phases while frozen. Re-assess only if a future kiro release surfaces a rate-limit/plan/credit field in the tailed .jsonl (or a kiro statusline POST hook lands) AND the kiro adapter adds matching ingestion. Observations: the kiro JSONL carries no model/token/cost.
